target.title: [codex] Fix memory recall prompt registration
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the selected active memory plugin loaded during cold Codex/Copilot harness scoped plugin loads, so the memory prompt builder registration survives the late scoped registry load.
- Remove the Codex-local Memory Recall fallback text; Codex now uses the active memory plugin prompt builder instead of duplicating memory-core policy.
- Add regression coverage for registered memory prompts, no-builder/no-fallback behavior, and memory-slot activation filtering so workspace memory plugins are not auto-trusted by the harness load.

## Root Cause

Gateway startup loaded `memory-core`, but the later Codex harness scoped load for `openai/gpt-5.5` loaded only the harness/provider plugin set. That scoped load refreshes process-global plugin registrations, so the active memory prompt builder disappeared while the runtime/tool surface still exposed `memory_search` and `memory_get`. The prompt compiler then had memory tools but no registered Memory Recall section.

This fix includes the selected memory slot plugin in the scoped harness load after the normal activation/trust filter, rather than restoring a Codex-side fallback that could mask the same registration bug.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts` -> 12 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` -> 100 passed
- `pnpm build` -> passed after rebase onto `origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram `/new` on `@SalmeronDevBot` followed by the same memory-dependent question now compiles a prompt with the registered `## Memory Recall` section instead of exposing memory tools with no recall guidance.

Real environment tested: local systemd Gateway using `dist/index.js`, Telegram polling through `@SalmeronDevBot`, OpenAI provider `openai/gpt-5.5`, model API `openai-chatgpt-responses`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `pnpm build`
- `node dist/index.js gateway restart`
- `node dist/index.js gateway status --json`
- In Telegram: `/new`, then the same 77-character question through `@SalmeronDevBot`

Evidence before fix:
- Trajectory `96ef0d3b-22d8-46de-8bf0-630bdaea521e.trajectory.jsonl`, line 2
- `contextCompiledTs: 2026-06-12T04:15:13.684Z`
- `sessionKey: agent:main:telegram:direct:<redacted>`
- `runId: a963cc5a-3fa2-4283-90a3-d4ae9e828f02`
- `memoryRecallSectionCount: 0`
- `memoryToolsInPrompt: { memory_search: true, memory_get: true }`
- Log: `2026-06-12T04:15:10.784Z Inbound message telegram:<redacted> -> @SalmeronDevBot (direct, 77 chars)`

Evidence after fix:
- Gateway ready with memory plugin loaded: `2026-06-12T05:05:48.822Z http server listening (4 plugins: brave, codex, memory-core, telegram; 3.5s)`
- Telegram polling ready: `2026-06-12T05:05:50.035Z [default] starting provider (@SalmeronDevBot)`
- Runtime plugin prewarm complete: `2026-06-12T05:05:59.123Z agent runtime plugins pre-warmed in 79ms`
- Log: `2026-06-12T05:09:52.728Z Inbound message telegram:<redacted> -> @SalmeronDevBot (direct, 77 chars)`
- Trajectory `53873c9a-8e34-4e16-9818-f94d2714e825.trajectory.jsonl`, line 2
- `contextCompiledTs: 2026-06-12T05:09:55.305Z`
- `sessionKey: agent:main:telegram:direct:<redacted>`
- `runId: 4789badf-af6e-46d3-9e71-e7cca5fb3599`
- `memoryRecallSectionCount: 1`
- `fallbackHelperPhrasePresent: false`
- `memoryToolsInPrompt: { memory_search: true, memory_get: true }`
- `memory_search` succeeded at `2026-06-12T05:10:21.987Z` and `2026-06-12T05:10:27.144Z`
- `memory_get` succeeded at `2026-06-12T05:10:41.120Z`
- Telegram outbound succeeded: `2026-06-12T05:12:03.707Z telegram outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=<redacted> messageId=323 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text chunkCount=1`
- Session ended successfully at `2026-06-12T05:12:07.030Z`

Observed result after fix: The prompt contains exactly one registered Memory Recall section, memory tools are present and callable, memory calls succeed before the reply, and the Telegram response is delivered.

What was not tested: Crabbox visual recording and full CI/changed gates. The live proof used the local `@SalmeronDevBot` path requested for this repro.
